skill.name: gws-gmail-send
skill.description: Send emails through Gmail using the Google Workspace CLI. Supports attachments, HTML formatting, CC/BCC, draft saving, and send-as aliases.
skill.summary: Send emails through Gmail with support for attachments, HTML, CC/BCC, and draft saving.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: All 20 static analysis findings are false positives from Markdown code examples. The external_commands flags (15 occurrences) are bash documentation blocks showing gws CLI usage. The filesystem flags (3 occurrences) are relative path references in documentation links. This is legitimate documentation for a Gmail CLI tool with no executable code or security risks.

content.value_statement: Send emails programmatically through Gmail without leaving your terminal. This skill integrates Claude Code with the Google Workspace CLI to send emails with attachments, HTML formatting, and advanced options.
actual_capabilities: Send emails to one or multiple recipients with subject and body
Attach files up to 25MB total size with multiple attachment support

Format emails as HTML with fragment tags like

, , ,
Add CC and BCC recipients for controlled email distribution
Send from configured send-as aliases instead of the primary address
Save messages as drafts instead of sending immediately with --draft flag
limitations: Requires the gws CLI tool to be installed and authenticated with Google Workspace
Cannot send emails without user confirmation due to write command safety check
Attachment size is limited to 25MB total per email
HTML formatting requires fragment tags and does not support full <html>/<body> wrappers

use_cases.title: Automated Email Notifications
use_cases.description: Send automated notifications from scripts or CI/CD pipelines when deployment completes or tests fail.

use_cases.title: Batch Email Sending
use_cases.description: Send personalized emails to multiple recipients by combining Claude Code with CSV data and the gws CLI.

use_cases.title: Report Distribution
use_cases.description: Attach and send generated reports (PDFs, CSVs) to stakeholders automatically on a schedule.

prompt_templates.title: Send Simple Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an email to alice@example.com with subject 'Project Update' and body 'The project is complete.'
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic single-recipient email with plain text

prompt_templates.title: Send Email with Attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Send an email to team@company.com with subject 'Monthly Report' and body 'Please find attached the report.' Attach the file report.pdf

prompt_templates.title: Send HTML-Formatted Email
prompt_templates.prompt:
Send an HTML email to users@example.com with subject 'Newsletter' and body '

Welcome to our newsletter!


Feature highlights:
- New dashboard
- Faster performance'
prompt_templates.scenario: Rich text email with HTML formatting

prompt_templates.title: Send Email with CC, BCC, and Alias
prompt_templates.prompt: Send from alias@company.com to client@example.com with subject 'Proposal' and body 'Please review our proposal.' CC manager@company.com and BCC archives@company.com
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional email with multiple recipients and custom sender

output_examples.input: Send an email to alice@example.com with subject 'Hello' and body 'Hi Alice!'
output_examples.output: Email sent successfully to alice@example.com with subject 'Hello'

output_examples.input: Send an email with attachment to team@company.com with subject 'Report' and attach report.pdf
output_examples.output: Email sent successfully to team@company.com with subject 'Report' and 1 attachment (report.pdf)

output_examples.input: Save as draft: email to bob@example.com, subject 'Draft', body 'Testing'
output_examples.output: Draft saved successfully for bob@example.com with subject 'Draft'

best_practices: Always confirm with the user before executing send commands as this is a write operation that sends real emails
Use the --dry-run flag first to preview the request that will be sent without actually sending the email
Combine with --draft flag to save emails as drafts first for review before sending to recipients
anti_patterns: Do not send emails without explicit user confirmation due to the irreversible nature of email transmission
Avoid exceeding the 25MB attachment limit as larger attachments will cause the send operation to fail
Do not use full HTML document structure with <html> or <body> tags when using the --html flag

faq.question: Do I need to install anything?
faq.answer: Yes, you need the gws CLI tool installed and authenticated with your Google Workspace account before using this skill.

faq.question: Can I send emails to multiple recipients?
faq.answer: Yes, use comma-separated email addresses with the --to flag, or add --cc and --bcc flags for additional recipients.

faq.question: What is the attachment size limit?
faq.answer: The total size of all attachments per email is limited to 25MB. You can attach multiple files using multiple -a or --attach flags.

faq.question: Can I send emails from an alias address?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --from flag with a configured send-as alias. If omitted, the email sends from your primary account address.

faq.question: How do I save an email as a draft instead of sending?
faq.answer: Add the --draft flag to your command. This saves the message as a draft in Gmail instead of sending it immediately.

faq.question: Does this skill work with personal Gmail accounts?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Google Workspace accounts. Personal Gmail accounts may have limited functionality depending on your gws CLI configuration.
